_comment: polar-agent multi-tool config. Copy to ~/.polar/tools.json (or set POLAR_AGENT_TOOLS=<path>) and edit. Entries here OVERRIDE the built-in defaults by name; new names are appended. {{workdir}} and {{prompt}} are template variables substituted at spawn time. Streaming output to the operator terminal is automatic; capBuffer caps stdout/stderr at 200K/50K. POLAR_KIMI_TIMEOUT env var (or per-tool timeout_seconds) controls wall-clock cap; default 30 minutes.

tools._comment: kimi-cli (Moonshot). --quiet = print + final-message-only. --yolo = auto-approve. --continue resumes per-workdir session. The 'No previous session' stderr substring triggers a one-shot retry without --continue (first message in a fresh workdir).
tools.name: kimi

tools._comment: Anthropic claude. -p = print mode (non-interactive). --continue resumes the most recent conversation in cwd. bypassPermissions = the autonomous approval mode. We chdir into workdir explicitly so claude's per-cwd session resolution lands in the right place. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is read from inherited env unless overridden here. Uncomment the env block to force a per-tool proxy.
tools.name: claude

tools._comment: OpenAI codex. exec = non-interactive subcommand. exec resume --last reuses the most recent session. --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox = autonomous. Auth comes from CODEX_HOME (set by 'codex login'); no API key env var needed.
tools.name: codex

tools._comment: Custom tool example. Pretend you have a wrapper script /opt/myhelper that takes --cd and --continue flags. Per-tool timeout_seconds overrides the global default. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is fed from a literal value here (not recommended for real keys — leave that key out and let parent env carry it).
tools.name: myhelper
